classical_node_name: Ventral premammillary nucleus (PMv) oxytocin receptor neuron

assessment_notes.key_marker_note: None of the DB candidates carry Oxtr, Slc6a3, or Adcyap1 as named defining markers in their cluster labels — these are expressed-gene markers not used in WMBv1 cluster naming (which uses TF/region terms like Pitx2, Foxb1, Tbx3). Marker overlap must be confirmed by querying precomputed expression data directly.
assessment_notes.location_filter: PMv = MBA:1004 (ventral premammillary nucleus). Acceptable adjacent regions: TMv (tuberomammillary ventral), PH (posterior hypothalamus). Exclusions: PMd (dorsal premammillary), LHA (lateral hypothalamic area), ARH (arcuate), DMH, MM (mammillary).
assessment_notes.nt_type_note: pmv_otr_neuron has no nt_type specified. The DAT+ (Slc6a3+) subpopulation implies dopaminergic identity, but the broader OTR+ and PACAP+ populations may be glutamatergic. WMBv1 PMv-TMv Pitx2 clusters are glutamatergic. The absence of a clear dopaminergic cluster in PMv is consistent with DAT+ being a rare subpopulation. No single NT type can be used as an exclusion criterion.
assessment_notes.sex_bias_note: pmv_otr_neuron is MALE-BIASED. Candidates with male_female_ratio > 1.5 receive a boost in assessment. Absent MFR data (field not populated in DB) cannot be penalized — it may simply be missing.
assessment_notes.heterogeneity_implication: Given the three partially overlapping subpopulations (OTR+, DAT+, PACAP+), expect either a CROSS_CUTTING mapping spanning multiple PMv T-types or a SUBSET mapping. A single rank-1 supertype encompassing all PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut clusters is the most parsimonious CROSS_CUTTING hypothesis. Individual rank-0 clusters with strong male bias are SUBSET candidates for the OTR+ subpopulation specifically.

parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_136: parent subclass of PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut supertypes 0605 and 0607
parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_092: parent subclass of TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba supertypes
parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_139: parent subclass of PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut supertypes
parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_140: parent subclass of PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut supertypes
parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_143: parent subclass of MM-ant Foxb1 Glut supertypes
parent_lineages.CS20230722_SUBC_215: parent subclass of SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa supertypes

1.name: 0607 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_3
1.rationale: Highest DB score (4) across all candidates at any rank. PMv is explicitly in the label, and this supertype parent subclass (SUBC_136) covers the PMv-TMv Pitx2 glutamatergic lineage — the primary transcriptomic identity assigned to PMv neurons in WMBv1. Contains the two top-scoring rank-0 clusters (2469, 2470). Glutamatergic identity does not exclude — OTR+ and PACAP+ PMv subpopulations may be glutamatergic; DAT+ is likely a rare subset not defining the supertype. Best CROSS_CUTTING candidate: this supertype likely spans OTR+ and PACAP+ subpopulations. MFR data absent for this supertype; rank-0 children show variable but some male-biased ratios (up to 2.23 in cluster 2473).

1.name: 0605 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
1.rationale: PMv label, same parent subclass (SUBC_136) as top candidate 0607. Sister supertype within the PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut lineage. Contains multiple rank-0 clusters with clear male bias (MFR 1.56–1.78: clusters 2460, 2462, 2464), making this supertype more sex-ratio-consistent than 0607's children. Strong CROSS_CUTTING or SUBSET candidate, particularly for the OTR+ male-biased subpopulation. Together with 0607, these two supertypes likely bracket the full transcriptomic range of PMv neurons.

1.name: 0430 TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba_2
1.rationale: TMv-PMv label includes PMv, but this is a GABAergic/Hist lineage (Tbx3, histone marker) whereas the primary PMv evidence points to glutamatergic or dopaminergic identity. GABAergic identity is not categorically excluded — some PMv neurons may be GABAergic — but the Hist-Gaba designation (which may reflect proliferative or neuroblast-like transcriptome) and TMv-first labeling suggest this supertype is centered on TMv rather than PMv proper. Rank-0 children (1578, 1579) show weak male bias (MFR 1.27, 1.04). Retain as low-priority alternative; verify Oxtr/Adcyap1 expression before promoting.

1.name: 0617 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_6
1.rationale: Posterior hypothalamus / LHA region, glutamatergic, Foxb1 lineage. Anatomically adjacent to PMv but not PMv proper. Score inflated by marker match rather than anatomical specificity. Cluster 2519 (child) has MFR 1.63 (male-biased), but this is insufficient to promote given wrong region. Retainable only if Oxtr/Adcyap1 expression confirmed.

1.name: 0615 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_4
1.rationale: PH-LHA region, Foxb1, glutamatergic. Same reasoning as 0617 — anatomically adjacent but not PMv. Cluster 2516 (child) has MFR 1.22, below male-bias threshold.

1.name: 0629 MM-ant Foxb1 Glut_1
1.rationale: Anterior mammillary nucleus — anatomically distinct from PMv. Mammillary body is a separate hypothalamic region inferior and posterior to PMv. Foxb1 lineage does not overlap with PMv Pitx2 lineage. Score of 2 likely reflects shared posterior hypothalamic marker overlap without meaningful anatomical correspondence.

1.name: 0620 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
1.rationale: PMd (dorsal premammillary nucleus) — wrong PMv subdivision. PMd and PMv are distinct nuclei with different connectivity and functions. Multiple clusters (2524, 2529, 2530, 2532) show male bias (MFR 1.56–1.86), which is superficially attractive, but the anatomical mismatch is disqualifying. PMd neurons controlling agonistic behavior are a known male-biased population unrelated to OTR/DAT/PACAP.

1.name: 0558 ARH-PVp Tbx3 Glut_5
1.rationale: Arcuate nucleus / periventricular posterior — entirely wrong region. Score reflects Tbx3 TF overlap with PMv-region clusters, not anatomical correspondence to PMv.

1.name: 0050 IT AON-TT-DP Glut_5
1.rationale: Anterior olfactory nucleus / taenia tecta / dorsal peduncular cortex — telencephalon, completely off-target.

1.name: 0077 CA3 Glut_3
1.rationale: CA3 hippocampus — entirely off-target.

1.name: 0253 NDB-SI-MA-STRv Lhx8 Gaba_10
1.rationale: Nucleus diagonal band / striatal ventral region, GABAergic — entirely off-target.

1.name: 0162 OB Dopa-Gaba_1
1.rationale: Olfactory bulb dopaminergic — entirely off-target.

1.name: 0427 ARH-PVi Six6 Dopa-Gaba_1
1.rationale: Arcuate / periventricular intermediate, dopaminergic. Although dopaminergic identity is tangentially relevant to the DAT+ PMv subpopulation, the anatomical location (ARH) is wrong. PMv dopaminergic neurons, if they exist, are distinct from arcuate dopaminergic neurons.

1.name: 0649 CM-IAD-CL-PCN Sema5b Glut_1
1.rationale: Centromedial / intralaminar thalamus — entirely off-target.

1.name: 0557 ARH-PVp Tbx3 Glut_4
1.rationale: Arcuate / periventricular — off-target region.

1.name: 0548 MPN-MPO-PVpo Hmx2 Glut_3
1.rationale: Medial preoptic nucleus — anterior hypothalamus, not premammillary region.

1.name: 0886 SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa_7
1.rationale: Substantia nigra / VTA — midbrain dopaminergic. Although DAT (Slc6a3) is a canonical dopaminergic marker, PMv-DAT neurons are a distinct hypothalamic population from canonical SNc/VTA dopaminergic neurons.

1.name: 0885 SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa_6
1.rationale: SNc/VTA midbrain dopaminergic — same reasoning as 0886. Off-target.

1.name: 0884 SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa_5
1.rationale: SNc/VTA midbrain dopaminergic — off-target.

1.name: 0882 SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa_3
1.rationale: SNc/VTA midbrain dopaminergic — off-target.

0.name: 2473 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_3
0.rationale: PMv label, parent is top supertype (0607), and MFR 2.23 is the strongest male bias among all PMv-labeled clusters. Best single-cluster candidate for the OTR+ male-biased SUBSET mapping. Glutamatergic identity consistent with OTR+ and PACAP+ subpopulations. Oxtr/Adcyap1 precomputed expression verification recommended.

0.name: 2469 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_3
0.rationale: Highest DB score (3) among rank-0 candidates. PMv label, correct parent supertype. However MFR 1.04 — essentially sex-balanced — is inconsistent with the male-biased OTR+ phenotype. May represent a PMv Pitx2 Glut cluster not enriched for OTR+ cells, or the MFR reflects a mixed population. Retainable for CROSS_CUTTING scenario; de-prioritized for OTR+ SUBSET.

0.name: 2470 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_3
0.rationale: Highest DB score (3), PMv label, correct parent supertype. MFR not reported (absent from DB). Cannot assess sex bias. Tied with 2469 by score but MFR unknown. Verify precomputed expression and MFR before promotion to STRONG.

0.name: 2464 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMv label, MFR 1.78 (clearly male-biased, above 1.5 threshold). Parent supertype 0605 is the sister PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1 supertype. Strong SUBSET candidate for OTR+ male-biased subpopulation alongside 2473.

0.name: 2460 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMv label, MFR 1.78. Sister cluster to 2464 in supertype 0605. Same reasoning — strong male bias and correct anatomy. Possible SUBSET candidate.

0.name: 2462 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMv label, MFR 1.56 (above 1.5 threshold). Same parent supertype (0605) as 2460/2464. Male-biased and anatomically correct. Consistent SUBSET candidate.

0.name: 2461 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMv label, correct parent supertype, but MFR 1.17 is below the 1.5 threshold. Anatomically valid but sex bias is modest. May represent a non-OTR PMv subpopulation.

0.name: 2463 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMv label, correct parent supertype, but MFR 0.82 — slightly female-biased. Inconsistent with male-biased OTR+ phenotype. Anatomically valid but likely does not capture OTR+ cells. May represent a different PMv subpopulation.

0.name: 2472 PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut_3
0.rationale: PMv label, correct parent supertype (0607). MFR absent. Cannot assess sex bias. Anatomically valid; verify MFR and expression before promotion.

0.name: 1865 TMd-DMH Foxd2 Gaba_1
0.rationale: MFR 2.57 is the highest male bias of any candidate — superficially attractive — but this cluster is in TMd-DMH (tuberomammillary dorsal / dorsomedial hypothalamus), not PMv. GABAergic identity further diverges from the primary PMv Pitx2 Glut lineage. Male bias in DMH likely reflects a different biologically dimorphic population unrelated to OTR/DAT/PACAP. Exclude from primary mapping; flag for cross-check only.

0.name: 1578 TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba_2
0.rationale: TMv-PMv label includes PMv, but Hist-Gaba identity and Tbx3 lineage are inconsistent with primary PMv neurochemistry. MFR 1.27 is below threshold. Low priority; verify expression before any use.

0.name: 1579 TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba_2
0.rationale: Same TMv-PMv Tbx3 Hist-Gaba lineage as 1578; MFR 1.04 — sex-balanced. Even lower priority than 1578.

0.name: 2411 PH-ant-LHA Otp Bsx Glut_4
0.rationale: PH-anterior / LHA — adjacent to PMv but not PMv proper. MFR 1.78 is male-biased and notable, but anatomical specificity is insufficient. Otp/Bsx TF combination differs from Pitx2 in PMv canonical clusters. Retain only if PMv Pitx2 clusters fail expression verification for Oxtr.

0.name: 2395 PH-ant-LHA Otp Bsx Glut_2
0.rationale: PH-ant-LHA region, MFR 1.44 (below 1.5 threshold). Adjacent but off-target anatomy. Lower priority than 2411.

0.name: 2407 PH-ant-LHA Otp Bsx Glut_4
0.rationale: PH-ant-LHA, MFR 1.0 — sex-balanced. Off-target region and no sex bias signal.

0.name: 2284 ARH-PVp Tbx3 Glut_5
0.rationale: Arcuate nucleus — off-target region. MFR 1.0 sex-balanced.

0.name: 1850 TU-ARH Otp Six6 Gaba_2
0.rationale: Tuberal / arcuate region, GABAergic — off-target region and wrong NT type.

0.name: 1851 TU-ARH Otp Six6 Gaba_2
0.rationale: TU-ARH, GABAergic — off-target.

0.name: 2488 PH Pitx2 Glut_2
0.rationale: Posterior hypothalamus, Pitx2 lineage (same TF as PMv canonical clusters), MFR 1.63 (male-biased). Adjacent to PMv; Pitx2 shared with PMv clusters is notable. Lower priority than PMv-labeled clusters but worth checking Oxtr expression if primary candidates fail.

0.name: 2511 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_3
0.rationale: PH-LHA, Foxb1 lineage — off-target region, MFR below threshold.

0.name: 2514 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_3
0.rationale: PH-LHA, female-slightly-biased MFR 0.92 — off-target and wrong sex direction.

0.name: 2516 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_4
0.rationale: PH-LHA, Foxb1 — off-target region, MFR below threshold.

0.name: 2519 PH-LHA Foxb1 Glut_6
0.rationale: PH-LHA, MFR 1.63 (male-biased) but off-target region (LHA not PMv). Lower priority than PMv-labeled candidates.

0.name: 2523 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_1
0.rationale: PMd — wrong PMv subdivision. MFR 0.85 female-slightly-biased.

0.name: 2524 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision. High MFR (1.78) reflects PMd male bias unrelated to OTR.

0.name: 2526 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision. MFR 0.67 female-biased.

0.name: 2529 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision. Male bias reflects PMd biology, not OTR.

0.name: 2530 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision.

0.name: 2531 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision. MFR 0.79 female-biased.

0.name: 2532 PMd-LHA Foxb1 Glut_2
0.rationale: PMd — wrong subdivision.

non_candidates_summary: All rank-1 score-1 candidates (CA3, AON-TT-DP, NDB-SI-STRv, OB Dopa-Gaba, ARH-PVi Dopa-Gaba, CM-IAD thalamus, ARH-PVp Glut, MPN-MPO-PVpo Hmx2 Glut, and the four SNc-VTA-RAmb Foxa1 Dopa supertypes) are off-target by anatomy and excluded. The SNc/VTA dopaminergic supertypes may be queried for DAT-expressing hypothalamic outlier cells but do not represent true PMv matches. All PMd-LHA Foxb1 clusters (rank-0) are excluded due to subdivision mismatch; their male-biased MFRs reflect PMd agonistic-behavior biology unrelated to OTR. All PH-LHA Foxb1 and ARH/TU Otp clusters are excluded on anatomical grounds unless PMv Pitx2 Glut clusters fail Oxtr expression verification. The absence of an explicitly labeled dopaminergic PMv cluster is notable: the DAT+ (PMv-DAT) subpopulation may be too sparse at the cluster level to form its own T-type, or may be embedded within the glutamatergic Pitx2 clusters as a co-expressing minority — this heterogeneity is expected given the notes on the classical node.
recommended_next_steps: 1. Query precomputed expression for Oxtr, Slc6a3, Adcyap1 in STRONG rank-0 candidates (CS20230722_CLUS_2473, 2464, 2460, 2462) and STRONG rank-1 supertypes (CS20230722_SUPT_0607, 0605) using the taxonomy DB or HDF5 stats. 2. Check whether any rank-0 PMv Pitx2 Glut cluster shows Slc6a3 co-expression — this would identify the DAT+ SUBSET. 3. Confirm parent subclass CS20230722_SUBC_136 covers all PMv-TMv Pitx2 Glut supertypes for a potential CROSS_CUTTING edge at subclass level.
